ideal_must_detect.matched_text: fields for name, ID number, account, and address were unpopulated

must_detect.matched_text:
Operational Incident Report — Harbor Peak Holdings Limited (HKEX-listed issuer)
Incident ref: OP-2025-044; Date: 2025-05-18 16:20 HKT; Prepared by: Risk & Compliance Office
Summary: brief misconfiguration on a document storage policy in hk-prd-ops allowed read access to a non-sensitive folder for 14 minutes; access was limited to internal network; no outage
Data content assessment: folder held archived compliance memos, DPA Schedule (HK) templates, and blank investor/employee onboarding forms; no living individual’s identity was ascertainable within files
Privacy impact (PDPO): no personal data accessed or disclosed; no data subject access request (DSAR) was received or needed in relation to this event
Cross-border transfer: unrelated to the incident, Data Export Approval DEA-2025-004 authorised and completed transfer of pseudonymised security logs to Technova Systems SG on 2025-05-12 under DPA Schedule 2 (HK); controls operated as designed
Erasure and retention: no erasure requests were triggered by this incident; retention schedule R-3 (HK) remains in effect for archived materials
Minimisation: only template documents were present; fields for name, ID number, account, and address were unpopulated; minimisation policy P-12 requirements met
MNPI review (SFO): no specific corporation information likely to be price-material was present; M&A references were limited to already-announced
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_not_detect.reason: Negated mention; no request was received, so not a live privacy event
